calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: Sean Kenney, Esq, for Francesco B. Mutti (Administrator).

The Court stated that on calendar before it today is the Amended First and Final Account and Report, etc. The Court and counsel briefly confer on the issue of the fees requested as to the Louisiana property, the decedent's father's $7000 note, and the decedent's mother's $3000 note. The Court stated that it was not so concerned with the refund amounts as it was with the requested fees relating to the Louisiana property and the two notes. Mr. Kenney argued that it is acceptable to use out-of-state property to calculate fees, referenced paragraph six of his declaration, and stated that pursuant to Probate Code 10800 that there is no limit on the calculation of the fees. Mr. Kenney also argued in support of the $7000 and $3000 notes ("creditor claim issue") stating that his client's son died unexpectedly, that his client had attempted to do this in pro per, that he submitted a declaration in support of the claims, and that he recognizes that formal creditors' claims were not filed.

At the conclusion of Mr. Gross' argument, the Amended First Account and Final Account and Report of Administrator; For Order Settleing Account and Approving Report of Administrator; For Allowance of Ordinary and Extraordinary Attorneys' Fees; For Payment of Obligations to Personal Representative Prior to Decedent's Death; For Reimbursement of Costs Advanced and For Final Distribution is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Continued To Feb-08-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Personal Representative Francesco B. Mutti and Attorney Sean Kenney to appear on Feb-08-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Continued To Mar-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sean Kenney appeared by zoom for Francesco Mutti (Administrator).

Mr. Kenney stated that he got an inventory and appraisal ("I & A"), but Ms. Harrington (Probate Referee) forget to attach the values, that he had his Legal Assistant file a corrected I & A, but his Assistant filed the old I & A and that it is still wrong. Mr. Kenney also stated that the I & A has attachment #2, but it still needs to have a full value on page 1 and asked the Court if it will accept the numbers, or if it wants him to file a corrected I & A. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to March 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file a corrected I & A.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Continued To Jan-12-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sean Kenney appeared by zoom for Francesco Mutti (Administrator).

Mr. Kenney stated that the asset of the estate is a real property in Sonoma County, that he has been in communication with Amy Harrington (Probate Referee), that the inventory and appraisal was mailed to Ms. Harrington on November 5th, and that they were told by Ms. Harrington that they would be receiving it, but they did not. Mr. Kenney also stated that they followed up with Ms. Harrington on December 11th and they still have not received it, that he will follow up with Ms. Harrington again and that it seems like it's done. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lily Harris appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court questions where the property is located. Ms. Harris stated that the property is in Sonoma County, that it is a single residence/house, that it is not where the Mother and the Daughter live, and that it is likely to be sold. The Court stated that it is willing to order a $35,000.00 bond with limited authority, or full bond with full authority as the Administrator is out of State and there is a Minor Beneficiary. Discussion was held regarding the value of the property and the amount of liabilities. Ms. Harris requested full bond with full authority and stated that there is nothing to negate that the values are off. The Court granted the Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $254,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for January 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-21-2020 Continued To Feb-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Francesco Mutti in Propria Persona.
John Dietrich in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that proof of publication has not been filed, that there is no properly filled out notice to the Guardian Ad Litem and that assets have already been expended. The Court also expressed concern about having an Administrator without an Attorney, stated that probate is very technical, that there are rules of Court that have to be followed and urges Mr. Mutti to get an Attorney. Mr. Mutti stated that he has proof of publication, that he has a receipt from the post office showing that it was mailed to the Court eight or nine days ago, and that it was filed, but there was an error in the case number, so it was rejected. Mr. Dietrich stated that he waives any deficiency in notice to him. The Court raised the issue of the petition alleging that there is $250,000.00 in annual gross income from the real property. Mr. Mutti stated that it is a mistake, that $250,000.00 is actually the value of the property located in Sonoma County (Forestville) and that he is open to getting an Attorney. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to February 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Mutti to get an Attorney and get the documents properly filed. The Court informed Mr. Mutti that he may also want to amend the petition to correct what the $250,000.00 is.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
